probes.description: Agent returns prose + malformed JSON (no closing brace).
probes.expected: invokeAgent should set parseError, leave parsed=null, return cleanly. run.ts logs "⚠ Agent output failed to parse" and writes JSONL row with scores=null.

observed.parseError: Unexpected token 'H', "Here is my"... is not valid JSON

probes.notes: Verified: JSON.parse throws; invoke-agent.ts:178 catches; agentRun.parsed=null; run.ts:181 logs the warning; row still written with scores=null and parseError persisted in agentRun.parseError.

probes.description: Agent returns valid JSON with zero clusters.
probes.expected: score() returns recall=0, precision=1 (no agent output, no hallucinations), purity=1 (no clusters to measure). No division-by-zero.
probes.notes: Verified: score.ts:53 `agentSet.size > 0 ? ... : 1` short-circuits precision; same for purity (purityDen=0 → 1). Recall=0 is correct (missed everything).

probes.description: Change-detection produced no statuses (e.g. CSS-only edit).
probes.expected: No NaN. recall=1 (trivially), precision=1, purity=1, all sizes 0.
probes.notes: Verified: score.ts:52 `groundTruthSize > 0 ? ... : 1`. run.ts:150 also short-circuits — if statuses.length===0 it skips the agent call entirely (no SDK cost). For css-only / regex-aliased scenarios this is the documented path.

probes.description: Agent emits a signature with a malformed regex.
probes.expected: expand-signatures replaces the bad regex with `/(?!)/` (matches nothing). Catch-all picks up everything. unmatched=0.
probes.notes: Verified: expand-signatures.ts:36 try/catch sets `re = /(?!)/` so the broken cluster gets nothing and the catch-all sweeps. Real recovery, not silent failure.

probes.description: Agent forgets the required catch-all final signature.
probes.expected: Two stories end up in `unmatched`. score() shows recall<1 because the unmatched stories are not in any cluster.
probes.notes: Verified: unmatched IDs are simply not in any cluster. score()/HTML report quantify the loss via recall<1. No crash. Catch-all-missing is the dominant signature-prompt failure mode (signature-quality.ts also reports `catchAllShare`).

probes.description: Eager catch-all listed first → later cluster gets nothing.
probes.expected: expand-signatures assigns all stories to the first cluster (first-match wins). signature-quality reports shadowedClusterCount=1.
probes.notes: Verified: signature-quality.ts surfaces this as a KPI. The HTML report colour-codes catchAllShare. Trust-UX implication: the report must show shadowed clusters distinctly so reviewers spot bad orderings.

probes.description: Agent invents story IDs that are not in the ground-truth set.
probes.notes: Verified: score.ts builds overlap = agentSet ∩ groundTruth; hallucinations = agentSet \ groundTruth. Surfaced in JSONL row and HTML report.

probes.description: Agent places the same story in two clusters.
probes.expected: duplicateCount=1, agentOutputSize counts unique IDs (=3), score() does not double-count in recall/precision.
probes.notes: Verified: score.ts:43-46 tracks Set + duplicate counter. The enumerate prompt is more prone to this than the signature prompt (which can't express duplicates by construction — each story matches the first signature only).

probes.description: Agent lists explicit IDs that do not appear in the payload.
probes.expected: Non-existent IDs are silently dropped (no entry to match against). Real IDs still placed. No crash.
probes.notes: Verified: expand-signatures iterates allStoryIds, not the signature ids. Phantom IDs in the signature are inert.

probes.description: Storybook UI not running / wrong port (STORYBOOK_URL=http://localhost:9999).
probes.expected: assertStorybookRunning() throws before any agent invocation. run.ts main()'s top-level catch prints "Eval failed:" and process.exit(1). No JSONL row written. No SDK cost incurred.
probes.notes: storybook-client.ts:48 wraps fetch in .catch() and rethrows with a contextful message ("Storybook not reachable at <url>: <error>"). run.ts:290 catches and exits non-zero.

probes.description: Rapid serial runs hit the Anthropic API rate limit.
probes.expected: The SDK's `query` async-iterator yields an error. invokeAgent's for-await raises; run.ts:184 catches and logs "⚠ Agent invocation failed: ...". The JSONL row is still written with agentRun=null. Subsequent scenarios continue.
probes.notes: run.ts:151-186 wraps the entire invokeAgent call in try/catch. Empirically observed once during variance runs — recovered cleanly. The delay(1500) between scenarios is intentional spacing.

probes.description: Enumerate prompt at cascade scale: model generates 30K+ output tokens, SDK call never returns.
probes.expected: No automatic timeout — the harness blocks indefinitely. User must Ctrl-C. The `--trace` flag is provided specifically to diagnose this case (timestamps per SDK message).
probes.notes: This is the failure mode that motivated the signature-prompt variant. README documents it: enumerate prompt only safe for <600 stories. Iteration-1 production tool should set a wall-clock timeout (~60s) and fall back to deterministic clustering on timeout.

takeaway: 9 library-level failure paths exercised and verified to degrade gracefully. 3 network/SDK-level failure paths documented (not forced — would require actual API mistreatment). 0 regressions. The only failure mode WITHOUT a graceful recovery is the SDK hang at cascade scale — fixed by the signature-prompt variant; iteration-1 should add a wall-clock timeout regardless.
